creator: mmel@FreeBSD.org
text:
I'm not sure I understand what "really shared" means in this context.
And I also think that I was too brief, sorry.

Imho, the problem is how is I/O window from primary side of bridge distributed/divided accross secondary ports.
Assume that I have uninitialized PCIe multiport root complex. When pcib_probe_windows() is executed for secondary ports, it allocates I/O window (from primary port resources) with its *maximum* size.  That mean that first secondary port consumes full I/O window (provided by primary port) and nothing left for others.
See attached dump (with following patch applied).

Anyway, the problem is harmless and because I don't see any real solution for it, it can stay on my endless TODO list forever :)

diff --git a/sys/dev/pci/pci_pci.c b/sys/dev/pci/pci_pci.c
index a61d8c2fb2e..3184d59d959 100644
--- a/sys/dev/pci/pci_pci.c
+++ b/sys/dev/pci/pci_pci.c
@@ -429,6 +429,9 @@ pcib_alloc_window(struct pcib_softc *sc, struct pcib_window *w, int type,
        if (type == SYS_RES_IOPORT && sc->bridgectl & PCIB_BCR_ISA_ENABLE)
                (void)pcib_alloc_nonisa_ranges(sc, w->base, w->limit);
        else {
+               device_printf(sc->dev,
+                   "allocate initial %s window: %#jx-%#jx\n",
+                   w->name, (uintmax_t)w->base, (uintmax_t)w->limit);
                rid = w->reg;
                res = bus_alloc_resource(sc->dev, type, &rid, w->base, w->limit,
                    w->limit - w->base + 1, flags);

**************************************************************************
